def: The quality of holding firmly to a purpose despite difficulty.
To make something seem less absolute by weighing it against other factors.
To support a claim with evidence or argument.
To examine an assumption critically rather than accept it.
To admit that something is true or valid; also to grant.
To follow and understand another's reasoning or feelings.
To make an idea clear by means of an example or image.
To make a vague idea specific and detailed.
To draw a broad conclusion from particular cases.
To hold something harmful in check and stop it spreading.
To stimulate activity or growth, as of an economy.
To deal successfully with a difficult task or problem.
To act against something in order to reduce its effect.
To cause something to happen.
To accept a drawback as the price of something else.
To cast doubt on something's truth or value.
To give due consideration to something.
To state one's position clearly on an issue.
To establish a benchmark that others follow.
To begin to become visible or apparent.
To show up as a visible result in something else.
To show its worth or reliability through experience.
To form the basis of something.
To consider or plan something for the future.
Being the factor that determines the outcome.
Very serious in its effects.
Having a wide and important effect.
Reaching deep and having a strong, lasting effect.
Absolutely necessary and impossible to do without.
Impossible to avoid or get around.
Having many layers or aspects; complex.
Making fine distinctions rather than treating things simply.
In a way that can be proven with evidence.
Believed to be so, but perhaps wrongly.
Strongly marked and clearly developed.
Widely used or accepted; current.
Sound and convincing, as a reason.
Following logically and leaving no doubt.
Having gaps; not full or thorough.
Typical and revealing of the nature of something.
Setting a direction for others to follow.
Having serious and far-reaching consequences.
Too great or too small in relation to something else.
Accepted by all; not open to argument.
In the same way; to the same degree.
In a way that can be noticed as it happens.
In a way that cannot be avoided.
From time to time; now and then.
To the greatest possible extent.
In the end; when all is considered.
Almost to the point of; virtually.
Generally; as is usually thought.
Before other things; as a priority.
Enough for the purpose; adequately.
Without being affected by; in spite of.
By using something as a guide or evidence.
For the benefit or advantage of.
With reference to; concerning.
Considering; in the face of.
As stated or implied by a source.
Coming from a particular person or group.
To make necessary; to call for as a duty.
The supreme ruler; in a democracy, the people as holders of power.
From now on; by now.
Not more than; possibly but no more.
To set fire to something.
To go to see a person or place for a purpose.
To invent or work out something in the mind.
To manage with what one has; also to get on with someone.
As an exception, not as the usual rule.
To work on or revise something.
Causing regret; unfortunate.
The act of touching; also contact with something.
As good as it can possibly be.
Now and then; occasionally.
To come through a danger or punishment without serious harm.
A strong desire to achieve success or distinction.
Having too high an opinion of oneself; also imagined.
To reach someone or something that was ahead; also to obtain.
To a moderate degree; reasonably.
To allow to enter; also to get involved in something.
To make something necessary.
The government office that collects taxes.
Bold, rude behaviour that shows a lack of respect.
Correct and official in manner; also literally.
A wooden string instrument played with a bow.
Currently valid or in effect.
A strong, selfish desire for more than one needs.
Words or a gesture used to greet someone.
So beautiful or charming as to sweep one away.
To say or put something more.
Whole and undamaged; in working order.
To join things with an adhesive; also to be sticky.
To put right a mistake or error.
Lasting for the whole of one's life.
To keep away from someone or something on purpose.
To reduce something to the smallest possible amount.
The period of European history between ancient and modern times.
Going on for several months.
Full of energy and good spirits; also wide awake.
In succession; one following the next.
Starting recently; as of late.
A large printed notice displayed in a public place.
The art of writing in verse; also a poetic quality.
The salty liquid the skin gives off when hot or exerting itself.
To force saliva or something from the mouth.
In an organised, methodical way.
A small portable electric light.
Not long ago.
To lose to an opponent; also to be governed by something.
Kept hidden; done in secret.
A picture made with a pen or pencil; also a marking pattern.
To return money that was borrowed.
Following of necessity; unavoidably.
At odd moments between other activities.
As a rule; in the normal way.
Material thrown away as no longer useful; also a decline.
A way of putting right a problem or complaint.
To state a detail; also to show off.
To bring about damage or trouble; also to arrange food.
To rouse someone from sleep.
Specially selected from among others.
To lose control of one's emotions (informal).
Merry and lively in a carefree way.
To make something larger in scope or extent.
Designed so that people with disabilities can use it.
To dishonestly offer money to influence someone; also to impress greatly.
A person who looks after or supports others.
The main software that runs a computer.
To like or choose one thing over another.
